title: Smoky Slate-Tile Baked Uruguayan Mixture Powder Bake
description: A cult classic Uruguayan dish featuring a mixture powder base, wok-hei, and a touch of natural sweetness. This recipe is a crowd-pleaser and suitable for those following a sugar-free diet.
instructions: Preheat the wok or large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the olive oil and cook the ground beef until browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the onion, garlic, and red bell pepper to the wok and cook until the vegetables are tender.
Stir in the mixture powder, tomato paste, salt, and black pepper. Cook for 1-2 minutes, until the mixture is fragrant.
Transfer the mixture to a 9x13-inch baking dish and top with breadcrumbs.
Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F for 25-30 minutes, until the top is golden brown and the mixture is hot and bubbly.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
Let the mixture rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
